title: Readiness vs liveness vs startup probes
description: Readiness controls traffic, liveness controls restarts, startup defers both for slow boots; misusing them causes outages.
body: Use when configuring or debugging pod health. Readiness failing removes the pod from Service endpoints but does not restart it; liveness failing restarts the container; startup gates the other two until the app has booted. A common outage is pointing liveness at a shared dependency: when that dependency blips, every pod fails liveness and restarts together, amplifying the outage. Do not use liveness for dependency health or omit startup on slow starters. Actions: keep liveness to a local wedged-process check, keep readiness reflecting serve-ability, set startup failureThreshold for boot time. Validation signals include no synchronized restart storms and traffic arriving only after readiness passes. Risk boundary: removing readiness from a serving workload changes traffic correctness and needs review.
